notes: Renamed iSeries 2000, then System i, then IBM i; still active as IBM Power Systems running IBM i in 2025
Integrated into AS/400 Integrated PC Server; evolved to Windows Server 2000+
Renamed i5/OS then IBM i; continues as IBM i in 2025
Intel Pentium Pro 200MHz board in AS/400 slot; original name FSIOP ('fizzy-op')
IBM's NOS on AS/400 prior to NT integration; OS/2-based
Third NOS option on Integrated PC Server alongside LAN Server/400 and NT
SQL Server, Exchange, Internet Exchange; Aberdeen notes AS/400 better for transaction processing
Web-enabled Notes; available on AS/400 Integrated PC Server May 1997; native mode expected year-end 1997
Key integration point between DB2/400 and NT applications
AS/400 integrated database; data transferred to NT apps via ODBC
IBM's Network Computer concept; cited alongside AS/400+NT as IBM's network computing vision
